name: csv_processor
description: Process CSV files (CSV only — never pass JSON files). Workflow tip: after transform_column produces JSON arrays in cells (e.g. '["transport"]'), search with 'contains' works on raw cell text — finish all filtering in CSV before converting to JSON with file_converter.

path.description: Absolute path to a CSV file or directory (must be .csv — use file_converter first if you have JSON)
Absolute path to a CSV file or directory (must be .csv — use file_converter first if you have JSON)

filters.description: Filters combined with AND logic. Always combine ALL conditions in a single search call — e.g. gender eq 'M' + city eq 'Warsaw' + birthDate gte '1986-01-01' + birthDate lte '2006-12-31'. All comparisons are string-based: gte/lte work directly on ISO dates without extracting year.

op.description: eq/neq are exact (case-sensitive). contains/startsWith/endsWith are CASE-INSENSITIVE — never repeat a search with different casing. gte/lte use lexicographic order (works on ISO dates like '1986-01-01').

transform_column.description: LLM-powered column transformation — only for semantic understanding, never for simple string/date ops

path.description: Absolute path to a CSV file (must be .csv — use file_converter first if you have JSON)

instructions.description: Natural-language transformation instructions. This is a 1:1 map — must produce exactly one output value per input row. Never ask to filter or skip rows here; use search for filtering.
